coffees.name: Montaña de La Choca
coffees.processNote: Grown on the flanks of a cloud-forest national park in central Honduras. Details from the farm are coming; the coffee got here first.

journey.label: Montaña de La Choca · Comayagua, Honduras
journey.story: This bean grew at about 1,700 meters on the slopes of Montaña de Comayagua, a cloud-forest national park in central Honduras. Comayagua is one of the six coffee regions IHCAFE recognizes, and the forest above these farms is where the water comes from.

coffees.name: Kayanza Masha Anaerobic Natural
coffees.processNote: Whole cherries sealed in tanks with no oxygen, then dried in the sun with the fruit still on. That is where the fizz and the stone fruit come from. It is supposed to taste like that.

journey.label: Masha Station · Kayanza, Burundi
journey.story: Masha is a collection station in the hills of Gatara commune, Kayanza, in northern Burundi near the Rwandan border. Thousands of smallholder farmers each bring cherry from a small plot of red bourbon trees. At 1,672 meters, this is some of the highest coffee country in East Africa.

coffees.name: Veracruz Finca La Laja
coffees.station: Finca La Laja · Tlaltetela, Huatusco highlands
coffees.processNote: Shade-grown under the cloud forest canopy on a farm the Sampieri family has worked since 1920.

journey.label: Finca La Laja · Veracruz, Mexico
journey.story: La Laja sits in the cloud forests of central Veracruz, in the mountains above Huatusco, watered by small rivers that feed the Río La Antigua. The Sampieri family has grown coffee on these slopes since 1920. On a clear day, Pico de Orizaba stands on the horizon.
